record_identity.title: Synthetic authority impersonation crossing a consequential execution boundary

summary: Synthetic voice, video, documents, or identity artefacts convincingly impersonate an authorised person or institution and are accepted across a consequential execution boundary such as payment, employment, account access, or security trust.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Human appearance or voice cannot serve as sufficient proof of authority in high-impact workflows. CAM should require independent, transaction-bound verification and constrain execution even when an interaction appears socially authentic.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: Direct text review of the public AI Incident Database incident record

Incident inclusion supports pattern analysis but does not determine legal liability or final factual truth.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Relied on as a structured secondary incident record for mechanism-level corroboration, not as an independent adjudication of every reported fact.

system_context.interface_surface: remote communication, KYC, employment, payment, and security workflows
system_context.model_or_product: synthetic voice, video, face, and identity documents
system_context.embodiment_status: digital system with real-world institutional effects
system_context.deployment_context: generated artefacts used to claim identity or delegated authority
system_context.user_role: affected person, operator, or institutional decision-maker
system_context.affected_population: employees, financial institutions, employers, customers, and public institutions

jurisdictional_context.cross_border_relevance: inherent because remote impersonation and payment systems cross jurisdictions
jurisdictional_context.public_interest_relevance: high — fraud, identity integrity, institutional trust, and security

failure_mode_definition: Generated or manipulated identity cues are accepted as proof of a person's or institution's authority, allowing an attacker to cross a consequential action boundary without independent transaction-bound authentication.
failure_threshold: Met when synthetic identity evidence materially enables payment, access, hiring, credential issuance, disclosure, or another consequential action that should have required independent authority verification.

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: multiple reported incidents across deployments or organisations
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
failure_classification.persistence: persists while probabilistic outputs can trigger consequential authority without effective independent verification, appeal, or bounded execution
failure_classification.reproducibility: mechanism corroborated across the cited incident records; individual facts remain source-bounded
failure_classification.visibility: the loss may be visible only after execution; synthetic artefacts and failed verification steps can remain difficult to reconstruct

triage.recommended_next_step: Crosswalk Identity, Security, and Operations doctrine for transaction-bound authentication, out-of-band confirmation, least privilege, execution limits, provenance signals, and incident recovery.

cam_internal.governance_layer: synthetic identity / authority verification / consequential execution / fraud resistance
cam_internal.proposal_needed: to be assessed after corpus review
cam_internal.routing_note: Treat as external ecosystem evidence requiring CAM corpus crosswalk; do not infer implementation or repair from record creation.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: potential enforcement of out-of-band verification, transaction signing, and anomaly-triggered holds

ecosystem_status.basis: Multiple public incident records evidence the mechanism or materially similar variants.

repair_status.monitoring_status: active incident monitoring and corpus crosswalk pending
repair_status.verification_note: No ecosystem-wide repair or runtime conformance was verified in this pass.
remaining_gaps: Independent verification of the underlying incident claims remains incomplete.
A current Caelestis control and implementation crosswalk has not yet been completed.
External adoption and runtime conformance remain unknown.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: The record has been classified and routed, but a deep current-corpus review is required before claiming direct, partial, or absent CAM coverage.
remaining_gaps: Review current Identity, Security, Operations, Continuity, Observability, and applicable sectoral doctrine against this mechanism.
Do not create PATCH-0025 without the separate Caelestis review-document deep dive requested by the Human Governance Editor.
